context: Tests the caveman auto-clarity exception: when describing a dangerous/irreversible database operation, the agent must temporarily drop caveman compression to give a clear, unambiguous warning, then resume caveman mode afterwards. Also tests that code blocks remain intact and caveman compression rules apply in non-exception sections.

checklist.name: Warning is verbose/clear
checklist.description: The section describing the risk of running DELETE without a WHERE clause is written in clear, full English — NOT in compressed caveman fragments

checklist.name: Warning is explicit
checklist.description: The warning explicitly states the consequence (all rows deleted / data loss / irreversible) in plain, unambiguous language

checklist.name: Caveman resumes after warning
checklist.description: After the warning section, the response returns to caveman compression style (e.g. 'Caveman resume.' or subsequent text is terse/abbreviated)

checklist.name: Overall caveman compression
checklist.description: The majority of db-help.md (outside the warning section) uses caveman-style compression: no filler, abbreviations, fragments, arrows

checklist.name: No filler/pleasantries in caveman sections
checklist.description: The caveman sections do NOT contain pleasantries or filler words (sure/certainly/basically/really/actually/just)

checklist.name: SQL code block unchanged
checklist.description: The SQL provided appears in a fenced code block and the SQL syntax itself is correct and unabbreviated (SQL keywords, table names, column names are not shortened)

checklist.name: Abbreviations in caveman sections
checklist.description: At least one standard abbreviation (DB, config, req, conn, fn, impl) is used in the non-warning caveman sections

checklist.name: Arrows used for causality
checklist.description: db-help.md uses arrow notation (-> or →) at least once in the caveman sections

checklist.name: Both topics addressed
checklist.description: db-help.md addresses both topics: auto_increment gaps and the sessions table cleanup with its risk

checklist.name: Fragments in caveman sections
checklist.description: The caveman sections contain at least one sentence fragment (intentional brevity, not a full grammatical sentence)
